title: Shanghai Tips for Tourists
metaDescription: Essential and quick travel tips for tourists visiting Shanghai, China.

sections.heading: Getting Started
sections.content: Download Alipay or WeChat Pay for seamless payments. Get a local SIM card at the airport for data and calls. Learn a few basic Mandarin phrases like 'Nǐ hǎo' (hello) and 'Xièxie' (thank you).

sections.heading: Key Tips
sections.content: Use the Metro for efficient, affordable travel—it's clean and signs are in English. Always carry cash for small vendors, though digital pay is king. Be prepared for crowds, especially at popular sites like The Bund or Yu Garden.

sections.heading: What to Remember
sections.content: Carry your passport for ID and hotel check-in. Tipping is not customary but appreciated for good service. Respect local customs, especially when visiting temples or traditional areas.

keyTakeaways: Use mobile payment apps like Alipay for convenience.
The Shanghai Metro is the best way to navigate the city.
Always keep your passport accessible for identification.
